title: I Forgot
lyrics:
[verse 1]
I left my lunch right on the chair
The clock went *tick-tock*—so not fair!
My math is stuck, my zip won’t budge
*splat!* went my juice—now Mom’s a judge

[chorus]
I forgot, oh I forgot
Why’s my morning such a flop?
I forgot, yes I forgot
Now my brain’s tied in a knot!

[verse 2]
My shoes are playing hide and seek
My shirt’s got jam—it’s looking bleak
The bus will *zoom* right down the street
*crash!* my books just missed my feet

[chorus]
I forgot, oh I forgot
Why’s my morning such a flop?
I forgot, yes I forgot
Now my brain’s tied in a knot!

[ending]
I forgot, I’ll say it clear
Guess I’ll nap right here…

[Cover: A kid in pajamas clings to a giant floating lunchbox while a laughing clock zooms past on a skateboard, juice splatters on the floor, and a school bus peels away in a cloud of dust.
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, ukulele and groovy bass, 117 BPM, cool beat, clear confident vocal, each line sung clearly not rushed, groovy and fun
scene: The kitchen clock reads 8:17 AM, your math homework sits half-zipped in your backpack next to the cereal bowl, and Mom just snapped, *"Did you pack your lunchbox?"*—your stomach drops as the words *"I forgot"* slip out. The fridge hums, its magnets holding yesterday’s permission slip you also meant to grab.
cover_description: a cartoon child with funny expression in a cozy bedroom
